arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try SSQP/SymbolServer protocol formats
echo "=== Trying elf-buildid format ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying debug symbol format ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying uppercase buildid ==="
BUILDID_UPPER=$(echo "$BUILDID" | tr 'a-f' 'A-F')
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID_UPPER}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at various PC offsets within this library.

To symbolicate these frames, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with that specific BuildId
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or `ndk-stack` to resolve the addresses


3. The actual `.so` file with debug symbols or the .NET runtime debug symbol packages

I'm realizing that Microsoft publishes symbol packages for the Mono runtime through NuGet or their symbol server, so I could potentially retrieve the debug symbols for this specific BuildId from there. the Microsoft symbol server, the format for ELF files is typically `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-<buildid>/libmonosgen-2.0.so`, though there are variations. Rather than trying different symbol server URLs, I should check if tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` are available, or better yet, look for the debug symbols through NuGet packages since the .NET MAUI runtime symbols for arm64 Android would be distributed that way.

Without knowing the exact .NET version, I can't pinpoint the right NuGet package. Let me try constructing a symbol server URL using the build ID directly: `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/libmonosgen-2.0.so`. Though I'm realizing this might be test data with a fabricated build ID, so I should verify whether this is real or sample data first.

Looking at the directory structure with multiple tombstone files and a crashlog, this appears to be a test repository. Regardless, I'll try a systematic approach: first check for available symbolication tools like llvm-symbolizer or addr2line, then attempt to fetch debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server, and finally use those to symbolicate the stack frames.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try SSQP/SymbolServer protocol formats
echo "=== Trying elf-buildid format ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying debug symbol format ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying uppercase buildid ==="
BUILDID_UPPER=$(echo "$BUILDID" | tr 'a-f' 'A-F')
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID_UPPER}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1

toolArgs.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try SSQP/SymbolServer protocol formats
echo "=== Trying elf-buildid format ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying debug symbol format ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying uppercase buildid ==="
BUILDID_UPPER=$(echo "$BUILDID" | tr 'a-f' 'A-F')
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID_UPPER}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1

data.content: The symbol server has the files. Let me download them and symbolicate.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-a3fda937f721466785d9a93f94331506
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
mkdir -p symbols

# Download the debug symbol file
echo "Downloading debug symbols..."
curl -sSL -o symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"

# Download the binary itself
echo "Downloading binary..."
curl -sSL -o symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"

ls -la symbols/
file symbols/*
